text: Psychological maturity for marriage remains to be an important predictor for future family life. As the conditions for marriage are developing, it is vital that young couples who plan to live together for a long period of time are adequately prepared for their challenging roles as parents. In this study the relationship between psychological maturity for marriage and emotional intelligence in young men is investigated. 90 men, between the ages of 21 and 26 years, took part in this study. They completed the Psychological Maturity for Marriage Scale (SKALDOM II) and the Emotional Intelligence Questionnaire (INTE). This scale includes three components: self-acceptance, internal integration and world view, and attitudes toward marriage and family life. The men who scored high in the SKALDOM II were compared with men who scored low on this scale and it was shown that men with high psychological maturity for marriage had a significantly higher score on emotional intelligence then men with low maturity. The emotional competencies with the highest scores were self awareness, sensitivity for emotions of others and skill to control own emotions when dealing with problems in interaction situations. It can be assumed that emotional competencies are important skills in building strong relations. It is therefore comes highly recommended to get young men ready to face the future family and marital circumstances through learning and support in schools and in the families.
